ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Hɛrɔd funɛ sou, Koosa malaika ti fun bur wa Josɛf wati a li Ijipt pulum ni.
translated: Herod died, and an angel told Joseph that he should leave Egypt.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Koosa la nyɛ kɔsa wa kilaarɛ. A wa bi a vusi ka limaŋ wa pisi, din yaa wa di a. O wati ma kaali kɔsa kinkan. O wa zi bu koti a nyɛ ma kilaarɛɛ? Ma se wa su un nɛnɛ.
translated: The monkey and the chameleon were friends. One day they went to the garden to harvest okra, which they were to eat. He told the chameleon to count the okra. Did he not know that he was going to count them? I don't know about that.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
text: Doone o gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun wa pɛɛsi un, “I funɛ biksi nara, baŋnɛ i fun jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ a nyɛ ba?”
translated: The monkey said to the tortoise, "I heard you are very cunning, is it true that you are very cunning?"

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Lɔsa funɛ kpak min, ma wa fun nyɛ min diwe mu di. Nyɔksa funɛ kpak min, ma wa fun nyɛ min ni mu nyɔ.
translated: If you love me truly, then come and eat my food. If you like me truly, then come and drink my water.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Lalaknɔ ba naa maga nyɛn Galili pulum ni fun nu chenani o tum. Yesu Fun Tiibi Nidamata (Mat.
translated: The Pharisees went to Galilee to see what Jesus was doing. Jesus Feeds the Five Thousand (Matt.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Hɛrɔd funɛ sou, Koosa malaika ti fun bur wa Josɛf wati a li Ijipt pulum ni.
translated: An angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream while he was in Egypt.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Koosa la nyɛ kɔsa wa kilaarɛ. A wa bi a vusi ka limaŋ wa pisi, din yaa wa di a. O wati ma kaali kɔsa kinkan. O wa zi bu koti a nyɛ ma kilaarɛɛ? Ma se wa su un nɛnɛ.
translated: The monkey and the chameleon were friends. One day they went to the farm to harvest beans, which they were to eat. He told the chameleon to pick first. But why did he not pick his friend first? I don't know that.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
text: Doone o gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun wa pɛɛsi un, “I funɛ biksi nara, baŋnɛ i fun jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ a nyɛ ba?”
translated: One day the tortoise went to the monkeys and said, "I have come, didn't you hear that I have become king?"

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.

translated: Kusa and Nyɛ were going to the farm. On the way, they saw a dove eating grains, and they killed it. He then asked Kusa to carry it. Why didn't he carry it himself? I don't know.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”

translated: When the chief's messengers came to his house and said, "Your friend has died, don't you want to go and see him before he is buried?"

translated: If you love me, let me come and eat your things. If you hate me, let me come and drink your water.
